title: China-to-Israel BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: BESS Fire Safety Installation — Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority Approval
rows.target_requirement: The Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority is the fire authority having jurisdiction for fire-safety requirements in Israel, alongside building-permit and local-authority processes. Stationary BESS installations should be treated as high-risk electrical and lithium-battery installations requiring project-specific fire-safety review before commissioning. The review may require fire-risk analysis, thermal-runaway propagation mitigation, gas detection or ventilation, firefighting access, emergency shutdown, separation distances, signage, and integration with building fire systems. Where Israeli Standards or project specifications adopt IEC 62933-5-2, the system-level safety file should align with that standard. Fire authority approval is a separate project gate from SII certification and grid connection.
target_standards: Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority — installation fire-safety approval and fire authority review
Local building-permit and authority approval process — project-specific gate for stationary BESS installations
IEC 62933-5-2 — Electrical energy storage systems — Safety requirements for electrochemical-based systems where referenced by Israeli Standards or project specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China manages BESS fire safety through product standards, system standards, and project-level fire review. Common China-side evidence may include GB/T 36558-2023 system documentation, GB/T 34120-2023 grid-related documentation, NB/T 42090-2016 PCS test evidence, battery safety reports, and local Chinese fire-review records. These documents do not replace Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority approval or Israeli building / installation review.
china_standards: GB/T 36558-2023 — 电力系统电化学储能系统通用技术条件 (General Technical Requirements for Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems in Power Systems)
GB/T 34120-2023 — 电化学储能系统接入配电网技术规范 (Technical Specification for Electrochemical Energy Storage System Connected to Distribution Network)
NB/T 42090-2016 — 储能变流器检测技术规程 (Technical Code for Testing of Energy Storage Converters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese fire-safety evidence and domestic fire approvals are not accepted as Israeli installation approval. Exporters and EPCs should: (a) engage the Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority, local building authority, and a qualified Israeli fire-safety engineer at concept design stage; (b) prepare a BESS fire-safety package covering thermal runaway, detection, ventilation, suppression, emergency response, access, and separation; (c) align the system hazard analysis with IEC 62933-5-2 where referenced; and (d) confirm any site-specific requirements before container layout, HVAC, suppression, and battery-rack spacing are frozen.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory project gate — installation fire-safety approval must be confirmed with the Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority and local building authorities per site
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, installation type, and local fire authority requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Israel fire-safety approval is a separate BESS installation gate. Chinese GB/T and NB/T files, and Chinese local fire approvals, do not satisfy Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority review. Confirm site-specific fire requirements early, including thermal-runaway mitigation, ventilation or gas detection, suppression, emergency shutdown, access, and separation distances before finalizing system layout.

source.publisher: Israel National Fire and Rescue Authority

rows.requirement: Israel Grid Connection for BESS — Electricity Authority, IEC, and 230/400 V 50 Hz Configuration
rows.target_requirement: Grid-connected BESS projects in Israel require review under Israel Electricity Authority regulation and the relevant grid / distribution connection process, commonly involving Israel Electric Corporation (IEC) or another licensed network operator. Israel has a strong utility-scale and distributed storage push, especially solar-plus-storage procurement and network-support projects, so project-specific tenders and connection agreements are central compliance documents. BESS PCS, protection settings, metering, communications, and dispatch capability must be validated for Israel's 230/400 V, 50 Hz low-voltage context and the applicable medium- or high-voltage connection point. Project specifications may require IEC 62933 series evidence, including IEC 62933-5-2 for electrochemical system safety and related system-level documentation.
target_standards: Israel Electricity Authority — grid regulation, licensing, and storage / renewable-energy tender framework
Israel Electric Corporation or licensed network operator connection agreement — project-specific technical gate for grid-connected BESS
IEC 62933-5-2 — Electrical energy storage systems — Safety requirements for electrochemical-based systems
Israel grid context — 230/400 V, 50 Hz; confirm voltage, protection, metering, SCADA, and dispatch settings per connection point
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS grid-connection documentation commonly relies on GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016. China's low-voltage grid context is typically 220/380 V at 50 Hz. Those documents may support supplier due diligence but they do not satisfy Israel Electricity Authority regulation, IEC / network-operator connection review, or project-specific solar-plus-storage tender requirements. PCS firmware, grid-code functions, voltage thresholds, protection settings, and communications must be rechecked for Israel.
china_standards: GB/T 36558-2023 — 电力系统电化学储能系统通用技术条件 (General Technical Requirements for Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems in Power Systems)
GB/T 34120-2023 — 电化学储能系统接入配电网技术规范 (Technical Specification for Electrochemical Energy Storage System Connected to Distribution Network)
NB/T 42090-2016 — 储能变流器检测技术规程 (Technical Code for Testing of Energy Storage Converters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese grid-connection approvals and type tests are not transferable to Israel. Exporters and EPCs should: (a) obtain the Israel Electricity Authority tender or regulatory conditions and the network operator's connection agreement before final equipment selection; (b) re-parameterise PCS voltage and protection settings for Israel's 230/400 V, 50 Hz system and the actual connection voltage; (c) confirm IEC 62933-5-2 and other IEC evidence requested by the project owner or network operator; and (d) validate metering, dispatch, cybersecurity, and SCADA / communication protocols required for the specific storage project.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory — grid-connected BESS requires project-specific Electricity Authority / network-operator connection review and approval
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS in China, but Chinese approval and 220/380 V settings are not transferable to Israel

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Israel grid connection is a project gate. Chinese GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016 grid evidence does not satisfy Israel Electricity Authority or network-operator approval. Configure and test the BESS PCS for Israel's 230/400 V, 50 Hz context and the actual connection point, and confirm IEC 62933-5-2, metering, dispatch, and communication requirements before procurement.

rows.requirement: Cell and Module Safety — SII Certification and Israeli Standards Adopting IEC Battery Requirements
rows.target_requirement: The Standards Institution of Israel (SII) is Israel's national standards body and certification route for products subject to mandatory Israeli Standards. For BESS cells, modules, battery packs, and associated industrial battery equipment, the applicable Israeli market-access path must be confirmed against current SII regulated-product scope and the project specification. In practice, Israel BESS projects normally require evidence aligned with Israeli Standards that adopt or reference IEC battery and electrical energy storage standards, including IEC 62619 for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications, IEC 62133 for portable sealed secondary cells and batteries where relevant to smaller battery assemblies, and IEC 62933-5-2 for electrochemical energy storage system safety. SII certification or formal confirmation that a product is outside mandatory SII scope is a gate to confirm before shipment.
target_standards: SII mandatory Israeli Standards and Standards Mark / product certification route — confirm current scope directly with SII before shipment
IEC 62619:2022 — Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes — Safety requirements for secondary lithium cells and batteries for use in industrial applications
IEC 62133 series — Safety requirements for portable sealed secondary cells and batteries where relevant to smaller battery assemblies
IEC 62933-5-2 — Electrical energy storage systems — Safety requirements for electrochemical-based systems
rows.china_equivalent: China-side BESS safety and system documentation is commonly prepared under GB/T 36558-2023 for electrochemical energy storage systems in power systems, GB/T 34120-2023 for distribution-network connection, and NB/T 42090-2016 for energy storage converter testing. These China standards can support technical understanding but are not SII certificates, are not Israeli Standards, and are not accepted as substitutes for SII certification or IEC-aligned evidence required by Israel project owners, conformity bodies, or grid reviewers.
china_standards: GB/T 36558-2023 — 电力系统电化学储能系统通用技术条件 (General Technical Requirements for Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems in Power Systems)
GB/T 34120-2023 — 电化学储能系统接入配电网技术规范 (Technical Specification for Electrochemical Energy Storage System Connected to Distribution Network)
NB/T 42090-2016 — 储能变流器检测技术规程 (Technical Code for Testing of Energy Storage Converters)
rows.gap: Critical gap: China GB/T and NB/T evidence does not establish SII certification or Israeli Standard compliance. Exporters should: (a) ask SII or the appointed Israeli importer / conformity body to confirm whether the BESS cells, modules, racks, PCS, and complete containerized system fall under mandatory Israeli Standard certification; (b) obtain IEC 62619 and, where relevant, IEC 62133 and IEC 62933-5-2 test evidence from an accepted laboratory; (c) map each supplied battery configuration to the exact certificate scope; and (d) avoid representing Chinese GB/T compliance as equivalent to SII approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the product falls under an applicable mandatory Israeli Standard or project specification; SII certification or formal scope confirmation must be verified per project before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on product scope and Chinese project approval; not transferable to Israel

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] SII certification and Israeli Standard scope must be confirmed before BESS shipment to Israel. Chinese GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016 documents are not substitutes for SII approval or IEC 62619 / IEC 62133 / IEC 62933-5-2 evidence required by the Israeli project route. Confirm SII scope and certificate coverage with the importer, project owner, and conformity body before production release.

rows.requirement: UN 38.3 Transport Safety Testing — Mandatory for Lithium Battery Shipments to Israel
rows.target_requirement: UN 38.3 (Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods — Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, Section 38.3) specifies mandatory transport safety testing for lithium metal and lithium-ion cells and batteries, including cells, modules, and battery packs used in stationary BESS. A UN 38.3 Test Summary is mandatory documentation under international transport regulations for lithium battery shipments. BESS cells and modules exported from China to Israel by air or sea must be covered by a valid UN 38.3 Test Summary and must meet the applicable dangerous-goods packaging, marking, labeling, and documentation rules for the selected transport mode.
target_standards: UN 38.3 — Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods, Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, Section 38.3
UN Model Regulations — lithium battery test summary, dangerous-goods classification, packaging, marking, labeling, and documentation framework
IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations and IMDG Code — apply according to air or sea transport mode
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese BESS cell and module manufacturers commonly hold UN 38.3 test reports and test summaries from accredited laboratories for export shipments. A Chinese-origin UN 38.3 Test Summary can be acceptable for Israel-bound shipments if it covers the exact cell or battery type, chemistry, capacity, and configuration being shipped and remains current after any design changes. China GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016 do not replace UN 38.3 transport documentation.
china_standards: UN 38.3 test reports and test summaries from accredited Chinese laboratories — acceptable for international transport if the summary covers the specific cell/battery type being shipped
GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016 — technical standards that do not replace transport dangerous-goods documentation
rows.gap: The transport gap is document scope, current validity, and dangerous-goods execution rather than national standard equivalence. Exporters should verify: (a) the UN 38.3 Test Summary covers the specific cell, module, or pack shipped to Israel; (b) design changes since testing do not invalidate the summary; (c) the shipment is correctly classified, packed, marked, labeled, and documented under IATA or IMDG requirements; and (d) the Israeli importer, freight forwarder, airline, shipping line, and port agent accept the documentation before dispatch.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under international dangerous-goods transport rules for lithium battery shipments to Israel by air or sea
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory under international transport conventions for lithium battery export shipments from China

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 transport compliance is mandatory for lithium BESS cells, modules, and packs shipped from China to Israel. Chinese-origin UN 38.3 summaries can be used when they cover the exact shipped configuration and remain current, but China GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 34120-2023, and NB/T 42090-2016 do not replace dangerous-goods transport documentation. Confirm classification, packaging, marking, labeling, and acceptance with the freight chain before dispatch.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) — Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods
